reasoning: Wei Yi's rating around mid-October 2025 is 2754. A 1% increase demands that he gains about 28 Elo points. For a super-GM rated over 2750, a 28-point gain is extremely unusual, effectively equivalent to a +2900 performance in a large tournament or sustained exceptional play across several consecutive events. The first two resolution dates will likely reflect the November FIDE list, providing virtually no time for such an increase unless he played and won almost every game in a late-October event (giving a probability near the minimum bound). The third date (Jan 24, 2026) gives about three months. While this allows more games to be played, leaping by 28 points to ~2782 would place him near the very top of the world ranking, a feat rarely accomplished so swiftly without a setback. Thus, the probability increases slightly over time but remains low.
